old_claim.note: Reed Mat Architecture provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No direct dependency remains because basketry granaries depend on weaving and agricultural storage practices, not the later scoped reed-architecture tradition.

source_shape.source_locator: Historical discussion of mudhif and reed architecture as a southern Mesopotamian architectural tradition.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source supports reed architecture as a building tradition, not as a necessary precursor for woven granary baskets in early farming settlements.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Basketry granaries should not depend on architecture; the relevant upstream capability is basketry/weaving plus agriculture, which remains elsewhere in the graph.

ontology_before: Basketry granaries carried a broad reed-architecture enabler edge that was actually an application-material association.
ontology_after: Basketry granaries keep agriculture context and no longer depend directly on reed architecture.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Graph invariant 2026-06-10-ancient-materials-scope keeps this removed edge absent and locks the corrected early-material chronology.
would_reject_if: The dependent node were narrowed to a later application that truly requires the removed prerequisite.
A stronger source showed the removed prerequisite existed earlier than and was necessary for the dependent node scope.
A future graph split introduced a narrower downstream node where this removed edge would be correctly scoped.
short_reason: No direct dependency remains because basketry granaries depend on weaving and agricultural storage practices, not the later scoped reed-architecture tradition.
